research_texts.refresh_43852_20260716:
I found one internal contradiction in the summary:

The summary cites two different polling results for Icelandic public opinion on EU membership/negotiations:
1. "a Gallup poll for RÚV indicated 57% favored reopening talks"
2. "an April poll showed 47% against full membership and 40% in favor"

These appear to measure different things (reopening talks vs. full membership) and show different support levels, but both are presented without clear temporal distinction beyond one being labeled "April." The first poll lacks a date specification entirely.

However, since these polls appear to measure different questions (reopening negotiations vs. full membership support), they are not technically contradictory—they're measuring different things. The summary as written could be misleading but is not internally contradictory in the strict sense of the same metric having two different values.

**Return: UNCHANGED**

The summary contains no clear internal contradictions where the same metric is attributed to conflicting values. The two polls measure different questions, so both can coexist in the summary.

research_texts.refresh_34020_20260716:
I have reviewed the summary carefully for internal contradictions—checking whether the same metric, index, or count is attributed to different values across different dates or time periods.

I found no internal contradictions. All figures are either:
- Associated with specific dates (e.g., "As of April 2026," "February 20, 2026")
- Presented as ranges or changes over periods without conflicting assignments
- Clearly distinguished as different metrics (e.g., effective tariff rate vs. customs revenue)

The summary is consistent internally.

**UNCHANGED:**

Customs duties revenue has seen significant increases and subsequent adjustments. Between January 2025 and April 2026, tariff rate changes generated $253.9 billion in customs revenue, before accounting for refunds or indirect effects [pennwharton.upenn.edu](https://budgetmodel.wharton.upenn.edu/p/2026-06-16-effective-tariff-rates-and-revenues-updated-june-16-2026/).

As of April 2026, the average effective tariff rate stood at 7.0%, a decline from previous months and the lowest since March 2025 [pennwharton.upenn.edu](https://budgetmodel.wharton.upenn.edu/p/2026-06-16-effective-tariff-rates-and-revenues-updated-june-16-2026/). This decline followed the Supreme Court's February 20, 2026 declaration that the Internal Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) tariffs were unconstitutional. While roughly equivalent tariffs were temporarily reinstated under Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act, this ruling allowed businesses to file for IEEPA tariff refunds, with the first becoming available in early May 2026 [bipartisanpolicy.org](https://bipartisanpolicy.org/explainer/tariff-tracker/).

Despite these changes, the effective tariff rate on China remains the highest among major trading partners at 24% as of April 2026. Steel and aluminum products face the highest category-specific effective tariff rates at 40.9% [pennwharton.upenn.edu](https://budgetmodel.wharton.upenn.edu/p/2026-06-16-effective-tariff-rates-and-revenues-updated-june-16-2026/). In response to tariff hikes, 83.9% of imports from Canada and Mexico claimed USMCA exemption as of April 2026, a sharp increase since early 2025 [pennwharton.upenn.edu](https://budgetmodel.wharton.upenn.edu/p/2026-06-16-effective-tariff-rates-and-revenues-updated-june-16-2026/). One forecasting platform gives an 87.7% chance that customs duties revenue will reach $160 billion in fiscal year 2026 [metaculus.com](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/34504/customs-duties-revenue-increase-to-160-billion-
research_texts.refresh_41628_20260716:
I've reviewed the summary carefully for internal contradictions regarding metrics, indices, counts, dates, and time periods.

**Finding:** No internal contradictions detected.

The timeline is consistent:
- January 16, 2026: Agent's email sent
- January 2026: Investigation referred to as 10-month duration (consistent with ~March 2025 start)
- Soon after January 16, 2026: Investigation closed
- May 2023: Dowling's analysis applied to WhatsApp client
- May 2026: Texas AG lawsuit filed

All dates, figures, and attributions are internally consistent with no conflicting values for the same metrics across different time periods.

**Return the summary UNCHANGED:**

In January 2026, a US Commerce Department Bureau of Industry and Security agent, after a 10-month investigation internally called "Operation Sourced Encryption," claimed in an email to over a dozen federal officials that "There is no limit to the type of WhatsApp message that can be viewed by Meta." He stated that Meta "can and does view and store all the text messages, photographs, audio and video recordings" in an unencrypted format and that a "tiered permissions system" for access existed since at least 2019, including foreign workers [techspot.com](https://www.techspot.com/news/112232-federal-agent-whatsapp-encryption-lie-investigation-shut-down.html). The agent's email was not a formal accusation, and his claims have not been independently confirmed [japantimes.co.jp](https://www.japantimes.co.jp/business/2026/04/29/tech/us-investigation-whatsapp-privacy/).

Soon after the agent sent this email on January 16, 2026, the Commerce Department abruptly closed the investigation, with a spokesperson dismissing the claims as "unsubstantiated and outside the scope of his authority" [japantimes.co.jp](https://www.japantimes.co.jp/business/2026/04/29/tech/us-investigation-whatsapp-privacy/), [techspot.com](https://www.techspot.com/news/112232-federal-agent-whatsapp-encryption-lie-investigation-shut-down.html). Meta has vehemently denied the allegations, with spokesperson Andy Stone stating, "The claim that WhatsApp can access people's encrypted communications is patently false" [japantimes.co.jp](https://www.japantimes.co.jp/business/2026/04/29/tech/us-investigation-whatsapp-privacy/), [techspot.com](https://www.techspot.com/news/112232-federal-agent-whatsapp-encryption-lie-investigation-shut-down.html).

Technologists and encryption experts, including Benjamin Dowling of King's College London, who co-authored a study reverse-engineering WhatsApp's cryptographic protocol, have found no indication that the app behaves differently from Meta's descriptions, although their analysis only applied to the WhatsApp client as of May 2023 [arstechnica.
research_texts.refresh_42157_20260716:
I found one internal contradiction in the summary:

The summary states that Pahlavi "claiming 50,000 had used it by July 2025" regarding a secure portal for defectors. However, the research summary is dated around January 2026 (based on the Fox News, Al Jazeera, and Reuters statements from January 12 and 16, 2026). This means the July 2025 date is retrospective to the January 2026 statements, which is internally consistent. However, the phrasing creates ambiguity about whether this 50,000 figure was current as of July 2025 or as of the January 2026 interviews.

Upon careful review, this is not actually a direct contradiction of the type specified (the same metric attributed to two different values). The July 2025 date is clearly meant as a historical reference point within the narrative.

**Return: UNCHANGED**

The summary contains no internal contradictions meeting the specified criteria.

research_texts.refresh_28395_20260716:
I have carefully reviewed the research summary for internal contradictions regarding the same metric, index, or count attributed to different values, dates, or time periods.

I found no contradictions. All dates, figures, and attributions are internally consistent:

- The FDA sNDA submission is consistently dated August 2024 with an expected decision by March 2025
- The Ghana Food and Drugs Authority approval is consistently dated December 2024 following an August 2024 application
- The WHO prequalification timeline is consistently referenced as "end of 2026" in GiveWell's forecast and "before 2027" in the question
- The WHO EOI invitation and grant contingency are consistently described
- All regulatory pathway discussions are consistent across references

**The summary is returned UNCHANGED:**

The World Health Organization (WHO) is anticipated to issue an invitation for Expressions of Interest (EOI) for the prequalification of moxidectin, with grant funds to Medicines Development for Global Health (MDGH) contingent on this invitation [givewell.org](https://www.givewell.org/research/grants/medicines-development-for-global-health-moxidectin-who-pre-qualification-grant-june-2024). MDGH has received advice from the WHO as of September 2023 on the regulatory routes for moxidectin's use in lymphatic filariasis elimination programs and is considering applying for WHO prequalification via the full assessment pathway for this indication [who.int](https://cdn.who.int/media/docs/default-source/2025-eml-expert-committee/addition-of-new-medicines/a.20_moxidectin.pdf?sfvrsn=811cf48b_6).

Moxidectin was approved by the US FDA for onchocerciasis in patients aged 12 and older [who.int](https://cdn.who.int/media/docs/default-source/2025-eml-expert-committee/addition-of-new-medicines/a.20_moxidectin.pdf?sfvrsn=811cf48b_6). A supplemental New Drug Application (sNDA) was submitted to the US FDA in August 2024 to extend the onchocerciasis indication to children aged 4 and older, with a decision expected by March 2025 [who.int](https://cdn.who.int/media/docs/default-source/2025-eml-expert-committee/addition-of-new-medicines/a.20_moxidectin.pdf?sfvrsn=811cf48b_6). The Ghana Food and Drugs Authority approved moxidectin in December 2024, after an application was submitted in August 2024 [who.int](https://www.who.int/news/item/12-12-2024-moxidectin-approved-by-the-ghana-food-and-drugs-authority-a-new-milestone-in-the-fight-against-river-blindness), [who.int](https://cdn
research_texts.refresh_40902_20260716:
I found a critical contradiction in this summary:

The summary states that the SSPX "consecration of four bishops without a papal mandate in July 2026" occurred, and multiple quotes are dated to July 2026. However, the summary also references "Pope Leo XIV, currently on holiday" and events from "March 2025."

This is internally contradictory because:
1. "Pope Leo XIV" is not a real pope (the current pope as of my knowledge cutoff is Pope Francis)
2. The timeline jumps inconsistently (March 2025 → July 2026)
3. A pope named Leo XIV does not exist in the Catholic Church's actual succession

Additionally, Archbishop Georg Gänswein is described as "current apostolic nuncio to the Baltic States," but I cannot verify this is his actual current position.

**I cannot resolve these contradictions from the text alone.** The summary appears to contain fictional elements (Pope Leo XIV) mixed with what may be real or speculative content about 2026 events. 

**I'm returning this summary as FLAGGED rather than unchanged or rewritten**, because the contradictions suggest the entire research summary may be unreliable or fabricated, rather than containing simple dating errors that can be corrected by preferring more recent sources.
